**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Nanjing Zhixin Micro-Circular Electronic Technology Co., Ltd. represents a classic specimen of organizational dimensional reduction: a "wafer fab by name, single-person studio in reality." The underlying, severely fictitious Apple Inc. supply chain and foundry network has been completely obliterated. The official industrial and commercial registration database appears to have been swayed by the profoundly deceptive grandiloquent naming of "Zhixin Micro-Circular" (intelligent chip and micro-wafer), directly categorizing it within the core hard-tech domain: "Manufacture of Computers, Communication and Other Electronic Equipment - Manufacture of Electronic Components." However, a forensic probe unsparingly exposed its physical reality: its paid-in capital is merely RMB 350,000, and its 2024 social insurance enrollment stands at "1 person." Its registered address is located at "Room 105, Building 2, No. 2 Shuanglong Street, Qinhuai District," a highly typical single-room office or ground-floor commercial unit. With RMB 350,000 and a single proprietor in Room 105, constructing a wafer fab to manufacture chips? Physics forbids it. The most crucial evidence lies within its business license, which, throughout its entirety, enumerates only "design, research and development, sales, and technical consulting," completely devoid of the terms "manufacturing" or "production." Diagnostic conclusion: this is fundamentally not a semiconductor manufacturing plant, but rather a "single-person fabless IC design studio" or a "reseller of electronic components and ceramic welded parts." The official "manufacturing" classification constitutes a systematic misjudgment, entirely detached from its licensed scope.
